approved_patterns.description: Layered surface container using token-governed depth, border, and shadow system. Creates spatial separation between content zones without decoration.
approved_patterns.allowed_use: Any content section requiring visual separation from the base surface. Must use surface and border tokens.
approved_patterns.accessibility_note: Background must maintain WCAG AA contrast with contained text. Panel type must not be communicated by color alone.

approved_patterns.description: Ordered list representation of the seven-layer Search-to-Action Journey. Semantic HTML structure with control-system styling applied as progressive enhancement.
approved_patterns.allowed_use: Homepage, SXO Framework route, and What Is SXO route only. Must render as readable ordered list without CSS.
approved_patterns.accessibility_note: Must be an <ol> or <ul> with numbered or labeled layers. Screen readers must be able to traverse the list.

approved_patterns.description: Directional connector using signal-color tokens to indicate journey flow between content sections or layers.
approved_patterns.allowed_use: Framework and methodology routes to show layer progression. Must be aria-hidden if decorative. Sequence must also be communicated through text.
approved_patterns.accessibility_note: If the path conveys sequence, sequence must also be communicated through numbered labels or ordered list markup.

approved_patterns.description: Governance transparency panel displaying route role, UX layer, and claim classification. Hidden by default in production.
approved_patterns.allowed_use: All routes. Default state is hidden. Only shown in development or operator review contexts.
approved_patterns.accessibility_note: Must use the hidden attribute when not shown. Must not interfere with primary content reading order.

approved_patterns.description: Structured assessment grid for scoring and audit surfaces. CSS Grid layout with fallback table/list structure.
approved_patterns.allowed_use: SXO Score and SXO Audit routes only. Must pass monetization gate before publication.
approved_patterns.accessibility_note: Score values must be expressed as text, not only color or visual indicator. Keyboard navigation through cells required.

approved_patterns.description: Stacked evidence and authority section using trust-color accent tokens. Renders citations, verified claims, and source references.
approved_patterns.allowed_use: Framework, methodology, and reference routes. Claims must pass claim classification gate before display.
approved_patterns.accessibility_note: Trust indicators must include visible text labels. Color is an enhancement, not the primary communication channel.

approved_patterns.description: Progressive enhancement motion pattern using CSS transitions to reveal content on scroll or interaction. Suppressed under prefers-reduced-motion.
approved_patterns.allowed_use: Panel entries, journey layer reveals, navigation state transitions. Must not hide content — only enhance its appearance.
approved_patterns.accessibility_note: Must be wrapped in prefers-reduced-motion suppression. Content must be fully readable before motion trigger.

approved_patterns.description: Governed navigation block using data from navigation.json. Semantic HTML with <nav>, <ul>, <a> structure.
approved_patterns.allowed_use: All routes. Navigation must derive from data/navigation.json, not be hardcoded.
approved_patterns.accessibility_note: Must use <nav> with aria-label. Active state must be communicated via aria-current, not only color.

approved_patterns.description: Full-viewport outer wrapper that establishes the diagnostic control grid background, base surface color, and depth context for all contained surfaces. Uses CSS grid-line tokens as background-image overlay with a radial signal gradient via ::before pseudo-element.
approved_patterns.allowed_use: Root body wrapper on all published routes. Must use --surface-control-base, --grid-line-soft, and --grid-line-strong tokens only. No external background images or textures.
approved_patterns.accessibility_note: Grid background must not interfere with text contrast. Signal gradient must remain decorative. overflow-x must be hidden to prevent horizontal scroll on any viewport.

approved_patterns.description: Sticky glass-surface command header using backdrop-filter blur and --surface-signal-field token. Contains the authority node (site identity), route cluster (primary navigation), and action node cluster (governed CTAs). Positioned at z-index 100 to float above the diagnostic content surface.
approved_patterns.allowed_use: Site header only. backdrop-filter must be the sole visual layering mechanism. Must remain functional without backdrop-filter support (fallback: opaque --surface-command background).
approved_patterns.accessibility_note: Must contain skip-to-main-content link as first focusable element. Navigation must use <nav> with aria-label. Must maintain WCAG AA contrast against both dark and light content visible through the signal-field surface.

approved_patterns.description: Layered control map rendering the seven-layer Search-to-Action Journey as a governed diagnostic surface. Each layer displays an index number, layer title, signal indicator, and description in a structured grid layout. Ambient radial glow via ::after pseudo-element communicates the active diagnostic state.
approved_patterns.allowed_use: Embedded as static HTML in page.html so it appears on all published routes. Must be readable as a plain ordered list without CSS. Grid layout and glow are progressive enhancements only.
approved_patterns.accessibility_note: Layer index numbers must be visible text, not CSS counters alone. Signal indicators must be aria-hidden. Each layer description must be readable without the adjacent indicator. Must not animate without prefers-reduced-motion guard.

approved_patterns.description: CTA and navigation action elements bounded by the route's monetization policy. Rendered as semantic <a> elements with --signal-blue border. Only appear on routes where monetization_allowed is true or where the CTA is listed in the route's allowed_cta field.
approved_patterns.allowed_use: Header action cluster and governed-cta-zone on pages where the route permits it. Must never appear on routes where monetization_allowed is false.
approved_patterns.accessibility_note: Link text must describe destination, not use generic labels. Focus ring must meet WCAG 2.4.11 minimum target size. Must not auto-trigger or appear only on hover.

approved_patterns.description: Governance transparency aside panel displaying route role, UX layer, claim level, monetization status, and publication status as a definition list. Hidden by default using the HTML hidden attribute. Uses --signal-violet left border for identification as a governance surface.
approved_patterns.allowed_use: All routes via route-context.html component. Must use HTML hidden attribute for default hidden state. Must not be visible in production without operator authorization. Content must derive from route data, not be hardcoded.
approved_patterns.accessibility_note: Must use <aside> with aria-label. When visible, must be keyboard-accessible and not disrupt reading order. hidden attribute ensures screen readers skip it by default.

approved_patterns.description: Governed content surface container for page body sections. Applies --surface-command background, 2px --grid-line-strong left border, and --radius-control for diagnostic panel identity. Wraps injected page content sections.
approved_patterns.allowed_use: Page body content area on all published routes. Must not contain inline styles. All surface, border, and radius values must reference CSS tokens.
approved_patterns.accessibility_note: Must maintain WCAG AA contrast between --surface-command and --text-primary. Left border is decorative — must not be the sole indicator of content type. Content must be readable without the container styles.

approved_patterns.description: CSS background-image grid using repeating-linear-gradient with --grid-line-soft and --grid-line-strong tokens at 48px intervals. Applied to the control-plane-shell to create diagnostic structure without decoration. Communicates the governed, structured nature of the control interface. No images, no canvas, no SVG.
approved_patterns.allowed_use: control-plane-shell only. Grid must be created using CSS gradient functions with CSS custom property tokens. No external image files, data URIs, or SVG backgrounds.
approved_patterns.accessibility_note: Grid lines must be sufficiently low-contrast that they never interfere with text readability. Grid is structural — it communicates system order, not decorative space imagery.

prohibited_patterns.description: Interface styled to resemble video game HUDs — health bars, XP counters, player status panels, game-world overlays.
prohibited_patterns.accessibility_note: Game HUD patterns are typically inaccessible and confusing outside gaming contexts.

prohibited_patterns.description: Heavy neon glow effects, cyan/magenta/purple neon borders, dystopian urban visual language, excessive glow blooms.
prohibited_patterns.allowed_use: Never. Subtle glow on signal-bearing elements within token constraints is permitted.
prohibited_patterns.accessibility_note: Neon-on-dark patterns frequently fail WCAG contrast requirements and cause eye strain.

prohibited_patterns.description: 3D avatar scenes, virtual world environments, social metaverse UI patterns, spatial audio interface metaphors.
prohibited_patterns.accessibility_note: Canvas-based 3D environments are fundamentally inaccessible to screen reader users.

prohibited_patterns.description: Primary content rendered only on HTML canvas without HTML text equivalent. Canvas used as sole content delivery medium.
prohibited_patterns.allowed_use: Never. Canvas may be used as progressive visual enhancement behind semantic HTML content.
prohibited_patterns.accessibility_note: Canvas content is invisible to screen readers and search engine crawlers. Violates static-first architecture and SEO gate.

prohibited_patterns.description: Navigation that requires animation to complete or reveal its targets. Menus that only appear after an animation sequence.
prohibited_patterns.allowed_use: Never. Navigation must be accessible and complete without any animation.
prohibited_patterns.accessibility_note: Animation-gated navigation is inaccessible to users with prefers-reduced-motion and breaks keyboard-only navigation.

prohibited_patterns.description: Text content hidden from users but visible to search engines via CSS tricks, tiny font sizes, or color-on-color techniques.
prohibited_patterns.allowed_use: Never. Constitutes a security and quality violation under SECURITY_POLICY.md.
prohibited_patterns.accessibility_note: Deceptive SEO practice. Violates search engine guidelines and may result in manual penalties.

prohibited_patterns.description: Three.js, WebGL, or CSS 3D scenes used purely for decoration without strategic content function.
prohibited_patterns.allowed_use: Never. All 3D must serve a documented content purpose and have a non-3D fallback.
prohibited_patterns.accessibility_note: WebGL scenes are inaccessible by default and impose performance costs without strategic return.

prohibited_patterns.description: Modal overlays or popups that interrupt content reading, block page access, or appear on a timer without user trigger.
prohibited_patterns.allowed_use: Never. Violates trust layer governance and MONETIZATION_BOUNDARY phase rules.
prohibited_patterns.accessibility_note: Intrusive modals create focus trap issues, disrupt screen reader flow, and violate WCAG 2.4.3.

prohibited_patterns.description: Low-trust promotional banners with urgency language (limited time, act now, only X left), countdown timers, or high-pressure visual design.
prohibited_patterns.allowed_use: Never. Violates CLAIM_POLICY.md blocked_claim classification and MONETIZATION_BOUNDARY phase rules.
prohibited_patterns.accessibility_note: Countdown timers and urgency animations are disorienting for users with cognitive disabilities.

prohibited_patterns.description: Traditional blog-style site header with logo-left, hamburger-right layout, no diagnostic depth, no glass surface, no authority node structure.
prohibited_patterns.allowed_use: Never. The command_header_interface pattern is the only approved site header form.
prohibited_patterns.accessibility_note: Prohibition is strategic. The command header must still meet all WCAG AA requirements.

prohibited_patterns.description: Navigation that collapses to an unusable, hidden, or truncated state below 768px without a governed mobile layout. Horizontal overflow on small viewports.
prohibited_patterns.allowed_use: Never. The route-cluster must use flex-wrap and appropriate responsive breakpoints.
prohibited_patterns.accessibility_note: Inaccessible navigation on mobile is a WCAG 2.4 failure.

prohibited_patterns.description: CTAs or action nodes that float, position absolutely, or overflow outside their governed-cta-zone or action-node-cluster containers.
prohibited_patterns.allowed_use: Never. All CTAs must be contained within governed zones.
prohibited_patterns.accessibility_note: Floating overlays violate WCAG 2.4.3 focus order requirements.

prohibited_patterns.description: Dark background applied without token governance, depth hierarchy, or diagnostic signal structure. Dark-for-dark's-sake styling that does not derive from the --surface-* token family.
prohibited_patterns.allowed_use: Never. All surface colors must reference the governed token system.
prohibited_patterns.accessibility_note: Generic dark surfaces frequently produce low-contrast text combinations.

prohibited_patterns.description: Gradients, glows, borders, shadows, or background effects not derived from the CSS token system in static/css/tokens.css. Inline styles, hardcoded color values.
prohibited_patterns.allowed_use: Never. All visual decoration must reference CSS custom properties from tokens.css.
prohibited_patterns.accessibility_note: Ungoverned decoration frequently produces contrast failures and inconsistent focus ring visibility.

prohibited_patterns.description: Interface elements that use cosmic, astronomical, or outer-space visual metaphors: star fields, nebulae, orbital rings, planet surfaces, galaxy textures, or space-station HUD patterns.
prohibited_patterns.allowed_use: Never. The interface represents a search-experience control system, not outer space.
prohibited_patterns.accessibility_note: Space-themed dark interfaces frequently produce contrast failures. The prohibition is primarily about strategic clarity and brand coherence.

prohibited_patterns.description: Use of orbit, deep space, galaxy, constellation, nebula, or astronomical language in class names, component names, design system terminology, or public-facing copy to describe interface elements that serve search-experience functions.
prohibited_patterns.allowed_use: Never. All terminology must derive from search-experience, diagnostic, or control-system vocabulary.
prohibited_patterns.accessibility_note: No direct accessibility concern. The prohibition is about conceptual alignment with the asset thesis.

prohibited_patterns.description: CSS classes, component names, or visual elements that use 'orbit' as a metaphor for circular motion, planetary rotation, or space travel when applied to search-experience interface components.
prohibited_patterns.allowed_use: Never as a space-travel or planetary metaphor. Sprint 10 orbit class names have been superseded by control-system equivalents in Sprint 10B.
prohibited_patterns.accessibility_note: No direct accessibility concern. Conceptual misalignment with sovereign search-experience positioning.

prohibited_patterns.description: Background grid or line patterns that are purely decorative and reference science fiction visual tropes (hex grids, targeting reticles, holographic displays) rather than the structured diagnostic control grid.
prohibited_patterns.allowed_use: Never as pure sci-fi decoration. The governed_signal_grid pattern uses a CSS background grid, but its function is to communicate diagnostic structure, not suggest sci-fi technology.
prohibited_patterns.accessibility_note: Decorative grids must not interfere with text contrast. The prohibition is about semantic framing.

prohibited_patterns.description: Multi-layer depth effects, parallax, or z-axis perspective that do not map to the information architecture of the search-to-action journey. Visual depth for dramatic or decorative purposes without communicating hierarchy.
prohibited_patterns.allowed_use: Never. Depth must communicate information hierarchy — which layer of the control interface the element occupies. Depth is diagnostic, not theatrical.
prohibited_patterns.accessibility_note: Excessive depth effects can create confusion for users with vestibular disorders. Parallax specifically triggers motion sickness concerns under prefers-reduced-motion.
